findingDetails.title: Fresh session can reap a daemon still serving a live secondary
findingDetails.detail: The release path keeps the daemon lease and drops only owner ownership, so a secondary can keep using the released daemon. A later fresh session sees the dead owner pid as staleReclaimable and reaps staleLease.childPid before spawning a replacement, with no gate for an active secondary or adopt-in-place path. The existing tests cover secondary survival and fresh-session reap separately, but not the combined case where the fresh session kills the backend that a secondary is actively using.

findingDetails.title: SIGKILL reap result is discarded before replacement spawn
findingDetails.detail: After the SIGKILL escalation path, reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn awaits waitForPidExit(childPid, 1000) but discards the boolean and returns allowed:true. A child that remains alive after the window, for example in an uninterruptible filesystem or WAL state, is treated as reaped and the launcher can proceed to spawn a replacement against the same database.

findingDetails.title: Comment hygiene misses reversed packet labels present in target launchers
findingDetails.detail: The checker blocks 'packet 096' but not '096 packet'. Both target launchers contain comments with '096 packet', and the checker exited clean on those files during this review, so the enforcement does not fully implement the no ephemeral packet labels rule.
